next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 5

Anya kept the Gesellschaft Club roster open across her knees as Damian brought the car in along the curb. Soap still clung to his collar over whatever cedar thing he'd put on and then tried to wash off before picking her up.

*She can probably smell that. It doesn't matter. Focus.*

She kept her eyes on the roster. Getting it had cost him something. She could tell from the way his thoughts kept catching and restarting. *Bretz? Over the phone? Absolutely not.* *Why do you need that list, Desmond?* *Forget it.* He had cut the calls off before curiosity turned into a report. He had not wanted to bring her. He had wanted that even less than he wanted to burn another favor.

Which was why she was here.

If the lead was real, it was moving too fast to hand off. If she was lying to him about something fundamental, better to keep her where he could see her.

"Bretz arrives around eight," Damian said, turning a page with the care of someone handling something more serious than a seating chart. "Cognac in the salon, dinner at nine, never stays past eleven. Tuesday is his procurement review, so if he's wound up about anything, it'll be tonight."

"And this couldn't wait until you found someone you actually trust."

His jaw tightened. "No."

That was at least honest.

"His wife is Margarethe," he went on. "She likes arranging people. If she decides you're decorative, you'll spend the night getting handed from one conversation to another. If she decides you're attached, you stay where she put you."

"So we go in attached."

"We go in as a couple because that's the only vers...
open_hooks_to_carry: A Renewal Front operative suspects Damian's loyalty — Anya reads this threat in real time
Anya's intervention saves Damian from exposure
Partial mutual disclosure: Anya reveals she is intelligence, Damian reveals he is running a counter-operation
Negotiated alliance terms: shared intelligence, no questions about networks, either can walk away
The double-deception weight: Anya cheated WISE for Damian, now negotiates with Damian using telepathy — every successful deception widens the gap between her and every person she deals with
prev_tail_excerpt:
At the entrance he offered his hand again.

"It was good to meet you, Anya."

"You too."

He held on half a beat longer than protocol required. Then, quieter, "If I remember where from, I'll tell you."

That one landed.

Anya kept her smile in place. "Please do."

He nodded and headed out into the cold with the look of a man carrying home a question he did not intend to drop.

Outside, the boulevard had emptied into the usual government-district dark. Streetlights on. Trees half-turned. Her wine buzz had burned off hours ago. All that was left was the cold and the day.

She had blown past the twenty-minute mark, let the gallery question stay alive, and kept taking the extra inch every time it opened. The lie to Sylvia had bought her room. She used all of it.

City thoughts brushed past as she walked toward the diplomatic quarter. Dinner. Rent. Wet shoes. A child reciting spelling words to herself. A man rehearsing an apology he was never going to make. The usual human spill, unasked for and easy.

At a red light she pulled one hand from her pocket and held it under the signal glow.

Steady.

She curled it back into a fist and waited for the light to change, with Sylvia's quiet "You've earned trust here" still in one ear and Damian's "Have we met before?" in the other.

The report was in WISE's system now. Her cover address was in Damian's pocket. Two lines she couldn't pull back.

When the signal changed, she crossed with everyone else, hand steady in her pocket.
state_deltas_required: Renewal Front operative pressuring Damian; Anya forced to intervene and partially reveal herself
The alliance forms on explicit terms. Anya carries the moral weight of two deceptions — her telepathic advantage and the false report that made the alliance possible.
